title: Trump Revokes CaliforniaГўв‚¬в„ўs Landmark Electric Vehicle Mandate, Setting Stage for Legal Showdown
content: In a White House signing ceremony, President Donald Trump signed three Congressional Review Act resolutions at the White House to dismantle CaliforniaГўв‚¬в„ўs electric vehicle (EV) mandatesГўв‚¬вЂќrules that had not only driven clean vehicle innovation in the state but had also been adopted by over a dozen other states aiming for aggressive climate action. These mandates required automakers to sell a certain number of zero-emission vehicles and meet stricter emission standards, making California a national leader in climate policy. Trump, backed by top Republican leaders and key department heads, justified the rollback by claiming it would rescue the U.S. auto industry from high compliance costs, restore consumer choice, and prevent California from having outsized influence over national vehicle standards. The move reflects a broader agenda to reduce environmental regulations and centralize authority at the federal level, but critics warn it undermines progress on climate goals, weakens state-level innovation, and risks slowing the adoption of electric vehicles just as global markets accelerate their green transition.
author: Analysts Account
